data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-05T00:55:07.246Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me recall what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations were never natively supported - they were always implemented as sync-over-async internally. In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), there was an opt-in mechanism to allow this pattern. In .NET 11 / EF Core 11, this sync-over-async support is being removed entirely.
 
The progression is clearer now - the Cosmos DB provider has always been async-only at the SDK level, so EF Core was blocking on async calls to support sync methods like `ToList()` and `SaveChanges()`. They started warning about this in EF Core 9, made it throw by default in EF Core 10 with an opt-in workaround, and in EF Core 11 that opt-in is gone completely, so any synchronous LINQ operations will fail. So the solution is straightforward—I need to migrate all the synchronous database operations to their async counterparts, replacing `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, `Single()`, and `SaveChanges()` with their `Async` versions throughout the codebase.
